description_long: This house features a sprawling, L-shaped layout with multiple rooflines and a combination of single and partial second-story sections. The exterior is finished in a mix of light-colored siding and stonework, with dark, steeply pitched gable roofs dominating the structure. Large windows are distributed asymmetrically, including prominent picture windows and smaller, regularly spaced units. The front facade includes a covered entryway with a modest porch, supported by columns and accented by a stone base. The house also features a visible garage section and several architectural projections, adding depth and complexity to the design. The overall style blends elements of Craftsman and modern suburban architecture, with a focus on comfort and curb appeal.
description: A large, L-shaped house with dark gable roofs, light siding, stone accents, and asymmetrical window placement. Features include a covered entryway, garage, and a mix of single and partial second-story sections.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the house from an angle highlighting the garage and the main entryway, with the dark gable roof and stone base visible.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side elevation, emphasizing the long, horizontal stretch of the house and the combination of rooflines.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the rear or secondary side, revealing more of the window arrangements and the L-shaped footprint.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a broad view of the back and side, showcasing large picture windows and the extended layout.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows and smaller units
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with a modest porch, stone base, columns, and overhang
